category_title: Verbal pupils who ignore or protest adult-led interactions
scenario_title: 1:1 playtime session with blocks and balls
minimal_context: The student is participating in a 1:1 playtime session with a classroom adult using blocks and balls.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: It is time for our 1:1 playtime session. We are going to play with the blocks and balls.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Blocks. (Asks for the blocks fairly fast to meet her own needs)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Here are the blocks. Now, stack the blocks up high.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Ignores the adult's directive and plays with the blocks on her own, showing no interest in interacting)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Look at the ball. Roll the ball to me.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: No! (Protests the directive communication, acting wilful and determined to have her own way)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is time for our 1:1 playtime session. We are going to play with the blocks and balls.

STUDENT: Blocks. (Asks for the blocks fairly fast to meet her own needs)

TUTOR: Here are the blocks. Now, stack the blocks up high.

STUDENT: (Ignores the adult's directive and plays with the blocks on her own, showing no interest in interacting)

TUTOR: Look at the ball. Roll the ball to me.

STUDENT: No! (Protests the directive communication, acting wilful and determined to have her own way)

task.content: 1:1 playtime session playing with blocks and balls

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When she lost interest in the motivating items or when adults used directive communication
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Protesting at a rate ranging from 0.42 – 4.6 incidences per minute, ignoring, and acting 'wilful'
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Did not like demands and expectations placed on them
related_quotes: she lost interest in them, so what would we need to do to move that forward?
She is very wilful, yeah I find that she is very wilful, it's her way or no way.

behavior_patterns.condition: When asked to work or when demands are placed
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Makes great efforts to meet her own needs, shows no interest in interacting, and is determined obviously to have her own way all the time
related_quotes: she was getting her way . . . She asked for them fairly fast, the blocks, the balls I suppose initially because she wasn't asked to work, emm she was getting a playtime.
